log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with GUI, and it has been detected as a spreader and overlay. I should investigate the network activity to see if there are any suspicious connections.
The network activity seems to be minimal, but I should investigate the registry changes to see if there are any persistence mechanisms.
The malware is using AppInit_DLLs to persist, which is a common technique. I should investigate the dropped files to see if there are any suspicious files written to disk.
The malware is dropping two executable files, zhsrrgd.exe and ctvqzym.exe. I should investigate the process activity to see if there are any suspicious processes spawned.
The notable modules list seems to be a list of legitimate Windows system modules. I should investigate the CAPA behaviors to see if there are any suspicious techniques used by the malware.
The malware is using Base64 encoding via WinAPI, which is a common technique. I should investigate the MITRE ATT&CK techniques to see if they match any known tactics, techniques, and procedures.
